title: Rivian Q1 2026 Earnings Analysis - Top Estimates but Caution Remains
text: Rivian exceeded Wall Street expectations on several metrics in Q1 2026. Revenue reached $1.381 billion with 11% year-over-year growth, beating approximately $1.37 billion estimates. Vehicle deliveries totaled 10,365 units, a 20% year-over-year increase. Adjusted EBITDA loss was $472 million, beating expectations of approximately $819 million loss. Consolidated gross profit reached $119 million, exceeding $56 million projection. However, significant concerns remain. Automotive gross profit swung to a $62 million loss from $92 million profit year-over-year, primarily due to a $100 million drop in regulatory credit sales. Free cash flow was negative $1.075 billion. Operating cash burn hit $703 million, up from $188 million year-over-year. Operating loss widened from $655 million prior year. The stock declined over 8% on Friday despite the earnings beat, suggesting beating the Street isn't enough when losing nearly $1 billion a quarter. Success hinges on the R2 model launch and an Uber partnership for up to 50,000 robotaxi vehicles. Full-year guidance targets 62,000-67,000 deliveries.
